title: Would I rather die among friends at 50 or alone at 80 - Request

transcript.text: So, video request, video request from Danemi.
Would you rather die peacefully among friends at age 50 or painfully than alone at age 80?
Assume that most of the last 30 years would be good ones.
That's easy, dude.
I'd die at 80 painfully and alone.
Because, number one, I get to experience more life.
Number two, I get to experience the world's changing.
The world's changing so much, it's worth it to stay alive just to watch the world change.
In 50, are you kidding me?
That's in like 11 years.
That's in 10 and a half years.
It's like right around the corner, dude.
Peacefully among friends, I mean.
Look, you're in pain all the time.
You go on a bike ride and your legs hurt.
Your pain's just part of being alive.
Alone?
Like, why is it so important to die among friends?
People almost never die among friends.
They die in accidents.
They die suddenly of like an illness.
You know, if you die of cancer, most of the time, I mean, I guess sometimes somebody would be there.
But if you're so out of it, it doesn't really matter.
Or, yeah, dude, I would die painfully and alone at age 80.
Yeah, I mean, even under like worst case scenarios.
Say I don't have any friends.
You know, all my family members are already dead.
I have no friends.
I go to a hospice.
People at hospices are friendly.
I could call them my friend.
And I wouldn't be alone.
I mean, suppose I go out on a hike.
And I'm going to die out in the woods.
Because I'm still not alone.
I've got all the birds and the trees and everything around me.
But I guess, you know, technically you'd say I'm alone.
And I'm in pain.
It doesn't seem, when you die, it's not really what's important.
It isn't really what, like, you know, it's not like who dies with the most toys wins, you know.
You don't, it's the mindset that you are, the spiritual kind of place your heart is in when you die.
That's what's important.
In Buddhism, it's really important to die.
The situation you are in when you die is really important.
But not, like, the physical situation.
It's the mental situation.
If you die at peace, and, you know, and your heart's in a good place,
It kind of, it's kind of like a motive, it's a momentum that carries you on into the next life.
For most people, you know, a lot of, there's sages who can die completely consciously.
And they're more conscious when they're, when they're dead to be able to direct themselves off into whatever life they want to reincarnate in.
But, but yeah, it's generally acknowledged in Buddhism that if you die, you know, in a peaceful mindset,
Then that peace will take you off and reincarnate you into a peaceful environment.
If you die in a tormented mindset, that'll, that'll kind of keep you like that, and you'll stay like that.
Kind of like, kind of like if you, if you watch a, a horror movie, and then you'll have nightmares that night.
So if you're having nightmares, and being dead is kind of like being, like dreaming, you know.
A lot of people don't know they're dead, when they're dead.
And so you're just kind of dreaming, and you don't know that, you know, you're just in this mindset,
And you don't know what's going on, and then you get, and then you get sucked, you know,
You gravitate towards like a situation that you're vibrating with, you know.
So, um, so it's important.
Um, I was going to say something else.
Um, uh, oh yeah, dream yoga, that's kind of a, a thing that, uh, my mom's been doing dream yoga for a long time.
Has a whole group of people who she does it with, and talks about it.
It's kind of like, you record your dreams, and then you, uh, do certain meditations to make,
Allow yourself to have lucid dreams, so that, um, when you die, you're used to remain lucid,
And can direct yourself, um, yeah, and drive yourself all over the spirit world,
And go exactly where you want to go.
Um, there's a lot of people, there's, you know, a tradition of sages in eastern cultures,
That people just choose to die, you know, they're just like, I die now, and then they die, you know.
And then those people are super lucid, so.
So, yeah, but I'd rather live a long age, because, I mean, I'd, I'd be dying in a more,
More peaceful mindset at the age of 80 than I would be at the age of 50, you know.
Like, people who commit suicide at young ages, um, you're kind of,
It's kind of like shooting a cannonball directly at a situation exactly like that, you know.
They'll get reborn right into a tormented situation.
Um, so, you don't fix anything by killing yourself, you fix stuff by fixing stuff.
Um, okay, that's it, peace out.
